Like, there were moments when I was feeling really down or uncertain, and they would just listen without judgment. It felt comforting to know that they accepted me as I was, flaws and all. I remember one time in particular, I was really stressed about a project, and a friend just said, “You’re doing great, and it’s okay to feel overwhelmed.” That simple acknowledgment made a huge difference. It reminded me that I didn’t have to have everything figured out to be worthy of support. I guess those moments felt like a safe space where I could just be myself without the pressure to perform or prove anything. But, honestly, it’s hard to recreate that feeling on my own. I often find myself wishing I could tap into that same sense of acceptance when I’m alone with my thoughts. It’s just... a lot to navigate, you know?

I start imagining all the worst-case scenarios, and it’s hard to pull myself back from that. I know it’s not always realistic, but it feels like I have to weigh every single option to avoid any potential fallout. It’s just... a lot. I don’t know if that’s normal or if I’m just overthinking it all.

topic_probe_events.client_reply: Mason: Yeah, I think you’re onto something there. It’s like my mind just gets stuck in this loop, replaying those moments over and over. I can’t help but think about what I could have done differently or how things could have turned out better. It’s almost like I’m trying to learn from the past, but instead, it just makes everything feel heavier. I mean, I know I can’t change what happened, but it’s like those thoughts just keep creeping back in, and I end up feeling paralyzed by them. It’s frustrating because I want to move forward, but it feels like I’m dragging all this baggage with me. I don’t know if that’s just how it is or if there’s a way to break that cycle.

I guess I put a lot of pressure on myself to manage my thoughts and emotions, almost like I’m responsible for keeping everything in check. But then, when it gets too heavy, I just want to escape from it all. It’s a bit of a mess, really. I know I should probably face those feelings, but the thought of doing that just adds to the weight. But at the same time, there’s a part of me that feels relieved at the thought of just being honest about what I need.
